finding: taskflow close can require runner-sync, but the closeback bundle excludes generated release artifacts and may leave runner-sync receipt files staged or release outputs dirty, creating an ambiguous post-close worktree.
expectedBehavior: When runner-sync is required for a framework task, closeback should either include a governed release artifact bundle and runner-sync receipt, or explicitly produce a follow-up work item with a clean index/worktree strategy.
evidenceOrRepro: ATM-GOV-0187 taskflow close succeeded at commit 1d836a0ef6ae7b4459092b4e8c153edee412f587, but release/atm-onefile and release/atm-root-drop files remained dirty and .atm/history/evidence/ATM-GOV-0187.runner-sync-receipt.json remained staged. record-commit dry-run accepted the receipt, but record-commit failed because release dirty made framework staging ambiguous.
followUp: Extend runner-sync closeback/scope admission to bind generated release artifacts and receipts to the steward work id, or update the workflow to create an explicit release-sync follow-up task before close.
